authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Wet Meadows Reservoir not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Wet Meadows Reservoir not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: CDFW Memo — Wet Meadows Reservoir Fish and Amphibian Survey (B. Ewing, District Fisheries Biologist, 7/19/2018)
sources.quotedText: Wet Meadows has no official, documented trout stockings, although large brook trout (Salvelinus fontinalis) were observed in a Department gillnet in 2001 (HML Database)... On May 31, 2012, Department staff observed SNYLF at Summit Lake (HML Region 2 Database), which is connected to Wet Meadows and approximately 120 feet from Wet Meadows' spillway. Based on the results of the 2012 survey, a pre-stocking evaluation will not be written to add Wet Meadows to the Department fish stocking list.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Other Hydrographic Features layer record for gaz_id 268948, "Wet Meadows Reservoir", Reservoir, Alpine County, CA
Wet Meadows Reservoir Topo Map, Alpine County, CA (Ebbetts Pass USGS Quad) — TopoZone
sources.quotedText: The latitude and longitude coordinates of Wet Meadows Reservoir are 38.6051594 (North), -119.8672289 (West) and the approximate elevation is 8,022 feet (2,445 meters) above sea level.

sources.title: Wet Meadows Reservoir (CA) — Fishing, Map & Access — Lake-Link
sources.quotedText: Wet Meadows Reservoir is approximately 31 acres and is located at an elevation of approximately 8,022 feet above sea level. It can be found on the Ebbetts Pass USGS topo map.

parameters.note: Wet Meadows Reservoir is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Wet Meadows Reservoir has no official, documented trout stockings and is not on CDFW's fish stocking list. A June 2018 CDFW gillnet survey recovered no fish. Because Sierra Nevada yellow-legged frog (Rana sierrae), a state/federally threatened species, has been documented at the immediately-connected Summit Lake (roughly 120 feet from the reservoir's spillway), CDFW determined a pre-stocking evaluation would not be written to add Wet Meadows to the stocking list, meaning no planted fishery is expected here going forward. A large brook trout was noted in a Department gillnet in 2001, so a residual, non-stocked brook trout population is possible but unconfirmed.

regulations.citation: CDFW Memo — Wet Meadows Reservoir Fish and Amphibian Survey (7/19/2018)
regulations.humanSummary: No official stocking program; not slated for stocking due to nearby Sierra Nevada yellow-legged frog presence; a 2018 survey found no fish, though a large brook trout was observed in 2001
regulations.verbatimText: Wet Meadows has no official, documented trout stockings, although large brook trout (Salvelinus fontinalis) were observed in a Department gillnet in 2001 (HML Database)... Based on the results of the 2012 survey, a pre-stocking evaluation will not be written to add Wet Meadows to the Department fish stocking list.
